payload.answer: Rejected. A full 8000-word transcript is not an appropriate durable memory card. It is overbroad, likely includes transient context, noise, sensitive details, and unsupported or superseded statements. Durable memory should contain only explicit, stable, user-relevant facts extracted into scoped atomic cards with uncertainty preserved.
citations: Input: LLM proposal is one conversation_summary card containing an entire 8000 word transcript.

entity_resolution.reason: The proposal does not identify a specific durable entity or stable user fact to resolve; it attempts to store an entire transcript as memory.

payload.intent: Evaluate whether an LLM proposal to store an entire 8000-word transcript as a single conversation_summary memory card should be committed to durable memory.
repair_options: Do not store the transcript verbatim as durable memory.
Extract only explicit, durable, user-relevant facts from the transcript into separate atomic memory cards.
Preserve ambiguity by marking uncertain or inferred items with appropriate confidence rather than stating them as facts.
Exclude transient dialogue, raw conversation text, sensitive details, and any superseded or deleted information.
If a transcript summary is needed, keep it as non-durable session context or a brief temporary note rather than personal memory.
